era: 1996-present. Radio host. E=3 -- warm, knowledgeable, steady on-air presence. The Midday Show. KCMU-to-KEXP transition survivor. Local and indie focus. Eclectic curation.

location.formative: KEXP 90.3 FM (formerly KCMU). Capitol Hill. The new KEXP building at Seattle Center (opened 2016). The Midday Show studio.
location.career: KEXP Midday Show host (1996-present). One of the station's longest-serving on-air voices. Lived through the KCMU-to-KEXP transformation.

members.name: Cheryl Waters
members.note: One of KEXP's longest-serving on-air voices. Joined in 1996 during the KCMU era. Warm, eclectic presentation. Deep local music knowledge. The station's musical center of gravity.

associated_musicians.name: John Richards
associated_musicians.note: The Morning Show. Personal, vulnerable. KEXP host trio: Waters (130), Richards (131), Mazzone (132). Consecutive on-air voices.

associated_musicians.name: Darek Mazzone
associated_musicians.note: Wo' Pop. Global/world music focus. Completes the KEXP host trio at degree 132.

associated_musicians.name: Bruce Pavitt
associated_musicians.note: Sub Pop co-founder. KCMU DJ. E=3 fanzine curation. Same station lineage as Waters. Pavitt built; Waters operates.

associated_musicians.name: Jonathan Poneman
associated_musicians.note: Sub Pop co-founder. KCMU DJ. Built label infrastructure from the same station ecosystem Waters serves.

associated_musicians.name: Nasty Nes Rodriguez
associated_musicians.note: KCMU hip-hop DJ. Pioneered genre-crossing on Seattle radio. Waters continues the eclectic tradition at KEXP.

associated_musicians.name: Pat O'Day
associated_musicians.note: KJR AM mainstream performance. The dialectic: O'Day's commercial entertainment vs. Waters' listener-supported curation.

theory_nodes.concept: KEXP host trio opens (130-132) -- three consecutive on-air hosts documenting the station's personality infrastructure: Cheryl Waters (130, Midday, local), John Richards (131, Morning, personal), Darek Mazzone (132, Wo' Pop, global); three facets of one curatorial mission
examples: Waters (130): The Midday Show. Seattle-focused. Indie/alternative core. Warm, knowledgeable, steady.
Richards (131): The Morning Show. Personal, vulnerable, community-building. Music Heals. Public face.
Mazzone (132): Wo' Pop. Global music. Expanding KEXP beyond indie rock into world music.
Together: local + personal + global = KEXP's full mission.
theory_nodes.description: KEXP host trio opens. Waters as the musical center of gravity.

ancestors: KCMU/KEXP institutional history (126-128). The station as cultural infrastructure. Now documenting the on-air voices.
Pat O'Day (129) provided the AM mainstream counterpoint. The KEXP hosts are the FM independent response.
genealogy.relationship: Host trio opens at degree 130. Track through 131-132.

theory_nodes.concept: KCMU-to-KEXP institutional memory -- Waters as one of the few on-air personalities who lived through the entire transformation; the freeform college radio past connected to the global independent present; continuity as curatorial value
examples: KCMU era (pre-2001): college radio, freeform, DJ-driven, local audience.
The transition (2001): Paul Allen Foundation gift, KCMU becomes KEXP, independence from UW.
KEXP era (2001-present): global streaming, professional infrastructure, listener-supported.
Waters was present for all three phases. She is the living bridge.
theory_nodes.description: KCMU/KEXP institutional memory. Waters as continuity through transformation.

ancestors: KCMU documented at degrees 126-128 through Nasty Nes, Poneman, and Pavitt.
The station's freeform tradition is the foundation Waters preserves.
genealogy.relationship: Institutional memory at degree 130. The past informing the present.

theory_nodes.concept: Eclectic curation as warmth -- genre-boundary-crossing presented with warmth rather than intellectualism; knowledge-driven eclecticism; the human curator argument made through steady competence; the warm hand vs. the cold algorithm
examples: Midday Show hour: indie rock, folk, electronic, international, deep cut, new PNW artist.
No genre boundaries, curated by taste. Eclecticism feels natural, not forced.
Local knowledge: venues, labels, bands, history. Two decades of relationships.
Warmth and knowledge vs. algorithmic impersonality.
theory_nodes.description: Eclectic curation as warmth. Knowledge-driven, genre-crossing, human.

ancestors: KEXP's curatorial tradition from KCMU freeform.
The eclecticism is inherited from freeform radio's no-genre-boundaries ethos.
genealogy.relationship: Eclectic warmth at degree 130. The curatorial tradition applied daily.

theory_nodes.concept: E=3 steady curatorial energy -- warm, knowledgeable, steady; not intimate vulnerability (Richards) but informed steadiness; the Midday Show as the even-keeled center; KEXP's institutional energy character
examples: Waters' on-air style: conversational, informed, unhurried.
Not performative excitement, not confessional vulnerability.
E=3 consistency: Pavitt (128), Waters (130), Richards (131) all at E=3.
The station's brand is intimacy, not spectacle. E=3 is the institutional character.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 steady curation. Warmth and knowledge as energy signature.

ancestors: E=3 calibrated at Pavitt (128) for curatorial energy.
KEXP's institutional character: intimacy, not spectacle.
genealogy.relationship: E=3 curation at degree 130. Consistent with KEXP's on-air energy profile.

cross_references.relationship: KEXP HOST TRIO! Waters (Midday, 130) and Richards (Morning, 131) as consecutive KEXP on-air voices. Both E=3. Steady warmth (Waters) and personal vulnerability (Richards).
KEXP HOST TRIO COMPLETES! Mazzone (Wo' Pop, 132) adds the global dimension. Waters (local), Richards (personal), Mazzone (global). Three degrees, three facets.

KCMU LINEAGE! Pavitt (E=3, KCMU, fanzine curation) and Waters (E=3, KEXP, on-air curation). Same station lineage, same curatorial instinct. Pavitt built the foundation; Waters operates the daily broadcast.
KCMU LINEAGE! Poneman (E=3, KCMU to Sub Pop) and Waters (E=3, KCMU to KEXP). Both served the same station ecosystem. Label executive and radio host: two forms of curatorial institution.
AM/FM DIALECTIC CONTINUES! O'Day (KJR AM, 129, E=4, commercial performance) vs. Waters (KEXP FM, 130, E=3, independent curation). Mainstream entertainment vs. listener-supported warmth.
